output_rule: Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, comments, or prose outside the JSON.

case_id.description: The case identifier associated with the claim.

auth_number.description: The authorization number recorded on the claim or associated payment record.

benchmark_source.description: The rate schedule source used for repricing.

benchmark_version.description: The rate schedule version used for repricing.

stale_source_rejected.description: The rate source that should not be used for the correction because it is stale or not applicable.

recovery_amount.numeric_precision: Round to two decimal places. Use the underpayment amount when the corrected allowed total is greater than the paid total.

lines.ordering: Sort by the claim line order from the environment.

basis_audit.description: Business basis audit trail. Record the source-precedence rule, the ordered record precedence trail, the environment record IDs that directly control the result, and the exception/gap records that explain exclusions, denials, missing information, or route priority.

controlling_record_ids.ordering_rule: Use the operational evidence order for the records that directly control the result.

exception_record_ids.ordering_rule: Use business gap/exception order: criteria or route gaps before stale or excluded records when both appear.

precedence_record_order.ordering_rule: List the controlling and exception records in source-precedence order, highest priority first.

cpt_code.description: CPT or HCPCS code for the claim line.

modifier.description: Use null when the claim line has no modifier.

correct_allowed_amount.numeric_precision: Round to two decimal places after applying units.

normalization_notes.currency: Use JSON numbers in dollars rounded to cents.
normalization_notes.modifier: Use null, not an empty string, when no modifier is present.
normalization_notes.line_order: The lines list must follow claim-line order from the source claim.

basis_audit.description: Business basis audit trail. Record the source-precedence rule, the ordered record precedence trail, the environment record IDs that directly control the result, and the exception/gap records that explain exclusions, denials, missing information, or route priority.

controlling_record_ids.ordering_rule: Use the operational evidence order for the records that directly control the result.

exception_record_ids.ordering_rule: Use business gap/exception order: criteria or route gaps before stale or excluded records when both appear.

precedence_record_order.ordering_rule: List the controlling and exception records in source-precedence order, highest priority first.
